name: RooCode
description: Open-source autonomous agent extension (Cline fork lineage)

developer.comment: Built by Roo Code, Inc. (RooCodeInc); originated as a community fork of Cline.

first-release.comment: Repo created 2024-10-31 as the 'Roo Cline' fork of Cline; later renamed 'Roo Code' (rename announced Jan 2025). Using initial fork publication month. Checked 2026-06-02.

status.comment: Discontinued: the VS Code extension, Roo Code Cloud and Router were shut down on 2026-05-15 and the GitHub repo was archived (isArchived=true). The team pivoted to a new cloud product, Roomote. The extension still functions but is frozen (no further fixes/model updates). Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Apache License 2.0 (OSI-approved) per repo license metadata.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent with built-in modes (Code, Architect, Ask, Debug, Orchestrator); not niche-positioned.

website.comment: Former product site roocode.com now redirects to roomote.dev (the successor product); GitHub repo used as the stable canonical link.

interface.comment: Distributed as a VS Code Marketplace extension (also a separate CLI component existed, but the agent is fundamentally an IDE extension).

host-ides.comment: VS Code (and VS Code-compatible editors via Open VSX). No JetBrains build.

open-source.comment: Agent's own source is Apache-2.0 licensed and public.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: A VS Code extension (plugin), not a standalone editor.

default-model-provider.comment: BYOK across many providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, AWS Bedrock, etc.) with no single bundled default model.

model-choice.comment: User picks among many providers/models within the extension.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Core model: user supplies their own provider API key.

local-model-support.comment: Supports local models via Ollama and LM Studio.

multi-file-editing.comment: Agentic coding tool that creates and edits files across the project.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Autonomous agent loop (edit, run commands, iterate) with optional auto-approval of actions.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell/terminal commands as part of its loop (with approval or auto-approve).

mcp-support.comment: Documented Model Context Protocol server support.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Orchestrator (Boomerang) mode breaks complex tasks into subtasks delegated to other modes via the new_task tool.

codebase-indexing.comment: Codebase Indexing builds a semantic embeddings index stored in a Qdrant vector DB (requires an embeddings API key); not just ad-hoc file reads.

planning-mode.comment: Architect mode is a planning-focused mode with edit restricted to markdown (read + plan, no code edits).

multimodal-input.comment: Supports drag-and-drop image input into chat (screenshots/mockups), subject to the chosen model supporting vision.

browser-computer-use.comment: Inherits Cline-lineage browser tool; can drive a browser to act/verify when used with a capable model.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational edits with per-action approval by default; can run fully autonomously when auto-approve is enabled. No tab inline-completion. A cloud layer added remote task control but the extension itself runs in-editor.

inline-completion.comment: Chat/agent-driven edits only; no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Roo Code Cloud added cloud agents with remote task control (dispatch/follow tasks from anywhere); that cloud layer was shut down 2026-05-15, leaving only the in-editor extension.

persistent-memory.comment: Relies on per-project rules files for context rather than learned cross-session memory; no documented persistent-memory feature.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Built-in checkpoints use a shadow git repo to snapshot before file modifications; restore files or files+task. Enabled by default.

git-integration.comment: Can run git commands via terminal execution; checkpoints use git internally. No deep native staging/commit UI beyond that.

pr-creation.comment: No documented native PR-creation feature in the extension; could open PRs only via terminal/CLI commands or an MCP server, not a built-in capability. Marked null per guideline (vendor silent, no independent confirmation).

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project-level .roorules instruction files (and AGENTS.md-style rules).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, custom modes, and slash commands.

permission-model.comment: Approval requested per action by default; auto-approve settings make it configurable toward auto-run.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source extension runs entirely on the user's machine and can run against fully local models (Ollama/LM Studio) with no vendor backend; the optional Roo Code Cloud was SaaS, not self-hostable.

privacy-mode.comment: Vendor stated 'we don't use your data for training' and the open-source extension can run with BYOK/local models so code need not reach Roo's servers at all; SOC2 Type 2 claimed. (Cloud service now discontinued.)

enterprise-controls.comment: Roo Code Cloud Enterprise plan offered SSO, RBAC, audit logs and SCIM. These were tied to the cloud offering, which was shut down 2026-05-15. Checked 2026-06-02.

pricing-model.comment: Extension itself is free and open-source (BYOK). The optional Roo Code Cloud added subscription tiers (Pro $20/mo, Team $99/mo) plus usage-based cloud-task hours; cloud now discontinued.

free-tier.comment: The extension is fully free (you pay only your own model provider via BYOK).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Extension is free for core use (BYOK); paid Roo Code Cloud Pro was $20/mo but optional and now discontinued. Using 0 per guideline since a genuine free tier covers core coding use.

maturity.comment: Was a polished, widely deployed tool (24k+ stars, 3M+ installs) — a 4 historically — but as of 2026-05-15 it is discontinued/frozen, so longevity is now nil. Rating reflects the product's realized maturity, not its future.

community-size.comment: ~24k GitHub stars and 3M+ installs at shutdown — medium scale (smaller than Cline). Community now dispersing to Cline/Kilo Code after discontinuation.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Meaningful ecosystem of MCP servers, community custom modes and rules; spawned forks (Kilo Code, ZooCode).

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md/AGENT.md (Agent Rules standard) plus Roo's own .roorules file and the preferred .roo/rules/ directory; also reads legacy .clinerules. Mapped to agents-md + custom (the .roo/.roorules/.clinerules conventions). Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: Global/user scope via ~/.roo/rules/ (%USERPROFILE%\.roo on Windows) and project/workspace scope via .roo/rules/ or root .roorules; workspace rules take precedence on conflict. No enterprise-admin policy layer or documented per-subfolder scope. Checked 2026-06-02.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Rules in .roo/rules/ are always applied. Conditional rule files exist only as mode-specific dirs (.roo/rules-{modeSlug}/), activated by the active mode — not by path/glob or file type. No path-based or file-type-based scoping, so tagged always-on. Checked 2026-06-02.

auto-memory.comment: Context comes from user-authored rules/skills files; no feature where the agent autonomously writes cross-session memory. Consistent with persistent-memory=false. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Dedicated Skills feature — task-specific instruction packages (SKILL.md) Roo loads on-demand — plus reusable custom slash commands and packaged custom modes. Beyond plain always-on instruction files. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills are model-callable — Roo auto-detects when a request matches a skill's description and loads SKILL.md. Custom slash commands provide the user-callable path (typed in chat). Both mechanisms exist. Checked 2026-06-02.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: only skill name + description are indexed by default; the full SKILL.md body is loaded via read_file when a match is detected, and bundled resources load on-demand. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills proper are file-based (created in ~/.roo/skills/{name}/SKILL.md or .roo/skills/, shared via version control / symlinks) — no skill marketplace or registry. Note: the Roo Code Marketplace offers one-click install but only for Modes and MCP servers, not Skills. Checked 2026-06-02.
